intro:
REEL TOPIC: Product Introduction
Cover in this order:
1. Hook — open with a compelling question or clinical scenario (e.g., "What if one tablet could address both nerve pain AND mood?")
2. Product name, brand positioning
3. Active ingredients with exact amounts (e.g., "Methylcobalamin 1500mcg + Pregabalin 75mg")
4. Dosage form (tablet type, film-coated, sustained-release, etc.)
5. Therapeutic category — what broad condition area it addresses

Duration target: 60-75 seconds (~150-180 words)
Scenes: 4-5 content scenes
indications:
REEL TOPIC: Indications — When to Prescribe
Cover:
1. Hook — "When should you reach for [product name]?"
2. Primary indications — the main approved conditions
3. Secondary indications — other approved uses
4. Typical patient profile — who benefits most
5. Key symptoms it addresses

Duration target: 60-90 seconds (~150-225 words)
Scenes: 4-5 content scenes
mechanism:
REEL TOPIC: Mechanism of Action — How It Works
Cover:
1. Hook — start with what the patient experiences, then explain the "why" behind the drug
2. For EACH active ingredient: explain its pharmacological mechanism clearly
3. Use simple analogies (e.g., "Think of calcium channels as gates that let pain signals through — Pregabalin closes these gates")
4. How the ingredients complement each other (synergy)
5. Why this combination is more effective than individual components

Duration target: 90-120 seconds (~225-300 words)
Scenes: 5-7 content scenes
dosage_safety:
REEL TOPIC: Dosage & Safety
Cover:
1. Recommended dosage and administration instructions
2. Key contraindications — who should NOT take this
3. Important warnings (e.g., driving impairment, pregnancy)
4. Special populations: elderly, renal impairment, hepatic impairment
5. Storage instructions if notable

Duration target: 75-90 seconds (~180-225 words)
Scenes: 5-6 content scenes
interactions:
REEL TOPIC: Drug Interactions & Precautions
Cover:
1. Hook — "Before prescribing, check for these interactions"
2. Major drug-drug interactions (name specific drugs)
3. Food interactions if any
4. Practical clinical precautions
5. What to monitor during therapy

Duration target: 60-90 seconds (~150-225 words)
Scenes: 4-5 content scenes
side_effects:
REEL TOPIC: Side Effects & Patient Counseling
Cover:
1. Common side effects (occurring in >1% of patients)
2. Rare but serious side effects to watch for
3. What to tell the patient before starting therapy
4. When to seek immediate medical help
5. Patient counseling tips

Duration target: 60-75 seconds (~150-180 words)
Scenes: 4-5 content scenes
